steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is an Inferences question asking for the most logical completion of the passage. The decisive clue is the contrast: lab studies control variables, but white-throated sparrows’ surroundings significantly affect their hormone levels. The phrase “not altogether surprising” signals that the blank must state the predictable outcome of that environmental sensitivity — a measurable difference in hormones between captive and wild sparrows.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — hormone levels were higher in wild males than in captive male degus.: Compares sparrows to degus rather than comparing captive sparrows to wild sparrows. The passage is specifically about white-throated sparrows, so a cross-species comparison is illogical here. ✗
  • Choice B — captive sparrows and wild sparrows usually exhibited very similar hormone levels.: Directly contradicts the passage’s premise that surroundings significantly affect hormone levels. Similar levels between settings would make the finding surprising, not “not altogether surprising.” ✗
  • Choice C — significant differences in hormone levels between individuals were found for both captive sparrows and wild sparrows.: Describes within-group individual variation, not between-setting differences. The passage sets up a captive-vs.-wild comparison, not an individual-vs.-individual one. ✗
  • Choice D — baseline levels of the hormone corticosterone are higher in captive sparrows than they are in wild sparrows.: Shows a concrete difference between captive and wild sparrows’ hormone levels — exactly what the passage predicts given that surroundings significantly affect hormones. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
